title: Add streaming output for real-time Morty progress
source: Anthropic API streaming (server-sent events) and Claude Code --output-format stream-json
description: Currently, delegate_to_agent() blocks on subprocess.run() with capture_output=True, meaning Rick (and the Rick Terminal) gets zero feedback until the entire agent completes — which can take 10+ minutes. Claude supports streaming responses, allowing real-time token-by-token or event-by-event progress that can be piped into roro events for live terminal updates.
proposed_changes: Replace subprocess.run() with subprocess.Popen() in delegate_to_agent() and summon_meeseeks(), using `claude -p --output-format stream-json` to get streaming JSON events. Read stdout line-by-line in a loop, parsing each event. Emit intermediate roro events (cto.morty.progress) when tool_use or text_delta events arrive. This gives the Rick Terminal real-time visibility into what each Morty is doing. Add a `stream=True` parameter to delegate_to_agent() with fallback to blocking mode.
